overall_justification: The article "O PADRÃO LEGAL" by Astrogildo Teixeira forcefully criticizes the Convênio de Taubaté and its proposed Caixa de Conversão, arguing from a clearly Orthodox perspective.  The author sees the Convênio as a threat to the ongoing efforts to redeem paper money and stabilize the currency, viewing the Caixa as a potential source of further inflation and a violation of the gold standard.  The article's prominent placement and strong language indicate the newspaper's endorsement of this view. While other articles mention topics tangentially related to the debate (e.g., immigration, which sometimes figures in Expansionist arguments), none directly address the Caixa de Conversão and its monetary implications with the same forcefulness or clarity as "O PADRÃO LEGAL." Therefore, this article sets the overall tone for the edition's stance on the debate.

supporting_evidence.quote: E' o que se verifica com o celebrado convénio de Taubaté, que antes e na impossibilidade de produzir qualquer beneficio veio perturbar a confiança depositada no resgate iniciado do nosso papel circulante e gerar o alarma sobre a nossa já complicada situação financeira.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote establishes the author's negative view of the Convênio de Taubaté, a key element of the Caixa de Conversão proposal, and its impact on currency stability.

supporting_evidence.quote: Para que resgotar papel com os sacrifícios do contribuinte quando elle pode ser recolhido ou seduzido na razão de menos de metade do seu valor por meio da conversão, á semelhança do que praticou uma nossa boa visinha?
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This rhetorical question reflects the Orthodox concern about the potential devaluation of existing paper money through the conversion scheme proposed in the Convênio.

supporting_evidence.quote: Acabar-se de uma vez por todas com essas arengas e sestros de fixidez do padrão monetário legal, substituindo-o por outro mais accommodaticio aos devedores.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote highlights the author's opposition to any deviation from a fixed monetary standard, a core principle of the Orthodox position, and suggests that the Caixa de Conversão is intended to benefit debtors at the expense of sound monetary policy.
